content.full_text:
I 
tate of CaliforIlia 
air Political Practices CommissioIL 
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
September 10, 1976 
Larry Milnes, Assistant City Manager 
City of Fremont 
City Government Building 
Fremont, California 
94538 
Dear Mr. Milnes: 
In reiteration of our conversation of September 8, 1976, 
please be advised that the advice your school district received 
from the office of the County Counsel of Alameda County is 
correct: 
The Board of Supervisors of that county is the code 
reviewing body for your district. 
If I can be of any further assistance to you please feel 
free to call me 
at 916/322-6444. 
DLS: jhm 
Sincerely, 
Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., 
Chief, Conflicts of Interest 
Division 


City of Fremont 
City Government Building 
Fremont, California 94538 
September 2, 1976 
Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr. 
Chief, 
Conflict of Interest Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Dear Mr. Spurlock: 
C 
9 331M '1' 
The City of Fremont was verbally advised by several FPPC staff 
members that the City Council of the City of Fremont would be the 
code reviewing body for the Fremont Unified School District, since 
the District is located wholly within the boundaries of the City 
of Fremont. 
We were subsequently advised by the Alameda County Counsel that the 
County Board of Supervisors is the code reviewing body for the Fremont 
Unified School District. 
A copy of the County Counsel's opinion is 
enc losed. 
Unless we receive written advice from the FPPC to the contrary, we 
intend to follow the advice of the County Counsel in this matter. 
'('jcerelY, 
~~ 
LARRY MILNES 
Assistant City Manager 
LM:dac 
cc: 
Wayne Ferguson, Superintendent, 
Fremont Unified School District 
Richard J. Moore, Alameda County Counsel

sections.conclusion: The Board of Supervisors of that county is the code reviewing body for your district.
sections.question_synthetic: Which entity serves as the code reviewing body for the Fremont Unified School District—the City Council of Fremont or the County Board of Supervisors—when the District is located wholly within the City's boundaries?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC affirmed that the Alameda County Board of Supervisors, not the City of Fremont, serves as the code reviewing body for the Fremont Unified School District.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (September 2, 1976) and the FPPC response (September 10, 1976). No formal Q&A section headers present; question and conclusion extracted from letter body. Email reference number '9331M' appears on request letter. Minor OCR artifacts visible in header ('CaliforIlia' for California, 'CommissioIL' for Commission).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Which entity serves as the code reviewing body for the Fremont Unified School District—the City Council of Fremont or the County Board of Supervisors—when the District is located wholly within the City's boundaries?

CONCLUSION: The Board of Supervisors of that county is the code reviewing body for your district.

embedding.summary: FPPC Chief Delbert Spurlock responded to City of Fremont Assistant City Manager Larry Milnes, confirming that the Alameda County Board of Supervisors is the proper code reviewing body for the Fremont Unified School District, contrary to earlier verbal advice from FPPC staff members suggesting the City Council would serve in that role.
